outline: Geometric Progression: n-th term, sum, sum to infinity

modules.title: Module 1 · Arithmetic Progression
modules.html:
An AP has a common difference $d$ added each term: $a, a+d, a+2d, \ldots$

$$T_n = a + (n-1)d,\qquad S_n = \dfrac{n}{2}\bigl[2a+(n-1)d\bigr] = \dfrac{n}{2}(a+l)$$

where $l$ is the last term.

modules.title: Module 2 · Geometric Progression
modules.html:
A GP has a common ratio $r$ multiplied each term: $a, ar, ar^2, \ldots$

$$T_n = ar^{n-1},\qquad S_n = \dfrac{a(r^n - 1)}{r-1}\ (r\ne 1)$$

subject: Mathematics
